summary: Systems architecture review accepts M4-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-CAPACITY-001. The implementation adds one narrow read-only query, sim.preview-m4-route-transport-capacity, reusing existing M2 route/season preview logic and existing M4 campaign, muster, and grain reservation state. The diff stays inside allowed paths, adds no commands, dependencies, save-format milestone work, UI, march execution, battle, siege, formal content, complex naval warfare, fleets, or amphibious behavior. Deterministic route tie-breakers, capacity bottleneck/overload reporting, travel-window fields, carried supply limits, monsoon/season risk reason ordering, and read-only behavior are covered by tests and verified by independent gates.

The writer REVIEW handoff and task JSON are tracked in the current branch and have no remaining working-tree diff.

tests_run.result: Task JSON parse/shape ok; status REVIEW; route_to systems_architect; risk R3.

decisions: ACCEPT. Scope is limited to the allowed protocol, sim-core, test, task, and handoff paths. No forbidden paths were modified.

The new query is suitable for this milestone because it is read-only, campaign-scoped by CampaignPlanId, and does not add a command, implicit RPC layer, persistent route-plan object graph, march execution, or generalized logistics manager.

Deterministic route selection delegates to the accepted M2 route preview path, which uses sorted directed edges and explicit route-id/direction tie-breakers. New M4 tests cover stable output across shuffled route and reservation ordering.

Capacity math is explicit at the forecast level: each carried grain source records routeSegments, bottleneckCapacity, carriedSupplyAmount, carriedSupplyLimit, overloadedReasonCode, and travelWindow; aggregate result reports carriedSupplyAvailable, carriedSupplyLimit, and the minimum per-source bottleneck.
Season and monsoon risks are stable reason codes sorted by explicit rank. Tests lock monsoon and road-delay ordering and confirm repeated queries produce identical results.

Player/AI parity is preserved because no new command is introduced. The query reads the same M4 campaign, muster, and grain state regardless of whether prior commands were submitted by player or AI through existing shared GameCommand paths.
River/water handling remains the accepted M2 routeKind seasonal capacity/cost modifier. The diff does not introduce fleets, naval battles, amphibious operations, or independent water-route state.
risks: This is an R3 protocol/query surface and should continue to qa_reviewer or the configured R3 downstream gate before lead marks the task ACCEPTED/CLOSED.

The aggregate carriedSupplyLimit intentionally sums per-source limits while aggregate bottleneckCapacity reports the minimum per-source bottleneck. This is acceptable as a planning forecast, but future simultaneous convoy execution would need a separate shared-edge capacity reservation model and ADR/task.
Forecasts currently support district campaign targets only. Polity-target route planning remains outside this task and should require an explicit target-to-district policy before implementation.

Save-format milestone work remains out of scope. The new query does not require save DTO changes, but future M4 save-runtime work must decide persistence compatibility for any M4 runtime fields it owns.
requested_action: Route this ACCEPT to lead_orchestrator. The implementation is acceptable from systems_architect review; proceed with the required R3 downstream QA/integration gate before marking M4-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-CAPACITY-001 ACCEPTED or CLOSED. Ensure the modified protocol/sim-core/test files plus the untracked new route-capacity test and this reviewer handoff are staged during integration.
